label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The problem is asking how many penguins are more than 5 years old and weigh more than 12 kg. Alright, so I need to check two conditions for each penguin: age greater than 5 and weight greater than 12 kg. First, let me list out all the penguins with their details. The table is given as: name, age, height (cm), weight (kg) Louis, 7, 50, 11 Bernard, 5, 80, 13 Vincent, 9, 60, 11 Gwen, 8, 70, 15 So four penguins total. Let me go through each one. Starting with Louis. His age is 7, which is more than 5. His weight is 11 kg. But 11 is not more than 12. So Louis doesn't meet both conditions. So he's out. Next is Bernard. His age is 5. Wait, the condition is more than 5, right? So 5 is not more than 5. So Bernard's age doesn't qualify. Even though his weight is 13 kg which is more than 12, since his age is exactly 5, he doesn't meet the first condition. So Bernard is out. Third is Vincent. Age is 9, which is more than 5. His weight is 11 kg. Again, 11 is less than 12, so he doesn't meet the weight condition. So Vincent is out. Then Gwen. Age is 8, which is more than 5. Weight is 15 kg, which is more than 12. So Gwen meets both conditions. So that's one penguin. Wait, but let me double-check. Are there any others? Let me go through again: Louis: age 7 (>5), weight 11 (<=12) → no Bernard: age 5 (not >5) → no Vincent: age 9 (>5), weight 11 (<=12) → no Gwen: age 8 (>5), weight 15 (>12) → yes So only Gwen meets both conditions. So the answer should be 1, which is option A. Hmm. But let me check again if I made a mistake. Wait, the question says "more than 5 years old and weight more than 12 kg". So age >5 and weight >12. So yes, only Gwen. But wait, Bernard is 5 years old. If the condition was 5 or more, then he would count. But it's strictly more than 5. So Bernard is exactly 5, so he doesn't qualify. So only Gwen. So answer A. But let me check the options again. The options are (A)1, (B)2, etc. But
